title: Why PlausiDen exists
description: Software written today should outlive its dependencies.

sections.title: We engineer for the next thousand years.
sections.lede: Not because we expect software written today to literally run for a millennium — but because designing as though it should produces fundamentally better software right now.

sections.text: Most stacks pick two or three. We refuse the tradeoff.

items.definition: No runtime database for content. Every page is HTML on disk; serving is a file read. The audit chain proves the same input produces the same bytes.
Merkle-chained signed reports + Ed25519 keys. Byte-identical builds across machines, across years. The CI doesn't sign off; the chain does.
Closed Rust enums + deny_unknown_fields at every JSON wire shape. Compile-time guarantees: if it builds, the categorical class of "runtime field-name typo" doesn't exist.
WCAG 2.1 AA + CSP-L3 + SLSA-3 enforced at build time. A regression doesn't ship; the audit phase rejects the commit.
Same Forge build, six deploy adapters: clearnet, Tor, I2P, IPFS, Gemini, Lokinet. Visitor picks the network; site doesn't care.
Zero CDN. Zero analytics. Zero third-party fonts. The browser makes the same requests every visit, no matter who the visitor is. The site can't see your visitors any better than you can.

sections.caption: Six axes. Every PlausiDen decision scores on all of them.

sections.body: We weren't trying to make AI go away. We were trying to make AI accountable. The Critic trait is the seam where 'the model said so' becomes 'these specific rules fired with these specific weights.'

sections.text: LFI is the brain. LLM is a constrained candidate generator. Every proposal flows through Critic::evaluate → Decision before any commit. The split:

sections.caption: LFI: the brain. LLM: the candidate generator.

items.value: Policy evaluation (NeuPSL weighted rules), similarity (HDC vectors), drift detection (VSA), brand-voice gating, abuse detection. Auditable + interpretable.
items.hint: Every Decision traces back to specific RuleId / Strength pairs an operator can read.

items.value: Fluent copy generation. Open-ended intent extraction. That's it.
items.hint: Everything else flows through the Critic before commit.

sections.body: We replaced three SaaS subscriptions with a single Forge build. The audit chain ships straight to compliance — and compliance stopped asking us follow-up questions.
sections.attribution: the kind of outcome the build pipeline targets

sections.body: Shipping a typed CMS in a week meant deleting the WordPress instance and never looking back. The reproducibility guarantee is the part that actually matters at year three.
sections.attribution: what the contract trades dependency-hell for

sections.title: Beta
sections.body: PlausiDen is in beta. Contracts are stable; runtime is hardening. Solo + Team plans are free during beta.

sections.title: Read the docs.
sections.lede: PlausiDen is built for teams shipping software they want to own in twenty years.
